content.json:
# 🌍 High-Risk Azure Control-Plane Changes by Origin

Maps the caller IP (`CallerIpAddress`) of **mutating** Azure management operations from `AzureActivity`, weighted by how dangerous the operation is. Bubble **size** and **color** = `RiskScore` (Critical = 10, High = 5, Medium = 1 per operation).

**The bubble to fear is a Critical operation — a role assignment, an `elevateAccess`, a deleted diagnostic setting — succeeding from a country you don't operate in.**

> Enriched with the native `geo_info_from_ip_address()` function. Geo data (MaxMind GeoLite2) is approximate — read as regions, not pinpoints. VPN and cloud-egress IPs resolve to the provider's datacenter, not the operator.

parameters.name: MinRiskTier
parameters.label: Minimum risk tier
parameters.jsonData: [{"value": "Medium", "label": "Medium and above", "selected": true}, {"value": "High", "label": "High and above"}, {"value": "Critical", "label": "Critical only"}]

content.query:
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where CategoryValue == "Administrative"
| where OperationNameValue has_any ("/write", "/delete", "/action")
| where isnotempty(CallerIpAddress)
| extend RiskTier = case(
    // CRITICAL - identity, entitlement, and defense-evasion operations.
    // These grant or hide access; a foreign origin here is an incident.
    OperationNameValue has_any (
        "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/ROLEASSIGNMENTS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/ROLEASSIGNMENTS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/ROLEDEFINITIONS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/ELEVATEACCESS/ACTION",
        "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/POLICYASSIGNMENTS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.SECURITY/PRICINGS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.INSIGHTS/DIAGNOSTICSETTINGS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.OPERATIONALINSIGHTS/WORKSPACES/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.SECURITYINSIGHTS/ALERTRULES/DELETE"
    ), "Critical",
    // HIGH - secret access and network exposure. Key Vault operations and
    // NSG/firewall changes are the classic pre- and post-exploitation steps.
    OperationNameValue has_any (
        "MICROSOFT.KEYVAULT/VAULTS/SECRETS/",
        "MICROSOFT.KEYVAULT/VAULTS/ACCESSPOLICIES/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.KEYVAULT/VAULTS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/NETWORKSECURITYGROUPS/SECURITYRULES/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/NETWORKSECURITYGROUPS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/AZUREFIREWALLS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/PUBLICIPADDRESSES/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.STORAGE/STORAGEACCOUNTS/LISTKEYS/ACTION",
        "MICROSOFT.STORAGE/STORAGEACCOUNTS/REGENERATEKEY/ACTION",
        "MICROSOFT.WEB/SITES/CONFIG/LIST/ACTION",
        "MICROSOFT.MANAGEDIDENTITY/USERASSIGNEDIDENTITIES/WRITE"
    ), "High",
    // MEDIUM - compute/data-plane manipulation and remote execution paths.
    OperationNameValue has_any (
        "MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/RUNCOMMAND/ACTION",
        "MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/EXTENSIONS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.SQL/SERVERS/FIREWALLRULES/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.SQL/SERVERS/DATABASES/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.STORAGE/STORAGEACCOUNTS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.LOGIC/WORKFLOWS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.AUTOMATION/AUTOMATIONACCOUNTS/RUNBOOKS/WRITE"
    ), "Medium",
    "Low")
| where RiskTier != "Low"
| where "{MinRiskTier}" == "Medium"
        or ("{MinRiskTier}" == "High" and RiskTier in ("Critical", "High"))
        or ("{MinRiskTier}" == "Critical" and RiskTier == "Critical")
// Strip any :port suffix on IPv4; leave IPv6 (colon-bearing, no port) intact.
| extend SourceIP = iff(CallerIpAddress matches regex @"^\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+:",
                        tostring(split(CallerIpAddress, ":")[0]),
                        CallerIpAddress)
// Azure-internal callers surface as literal tokens, not addresses.
| where SourceIP !in ("Azure Portal", "Internal", "PolicyEvaluation")
| where SourceIP matches regex @"^\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+$" or SourceIP contains ":"
| extend RiskWeight = case(RiskTier == "Critical", 10, RiskTier == "High", 5, 1)
| summarize RiskScore     = sum(RiskWeight),
            CriticalOps   = countif(RiskTier == "Critical"),
            HighOps       = countif(RiskTier == "High"),
            DeniedOps     = countif(ActivityStatusValue in ("Failure", "Failed")),
            DistinctIPs   = dcount(SourceIP),
            DistinctCallers = dcount(Caller)
| project pack_array(
      pack("Metric", "Total risk score",   "Value", RiskScore),
      pack("Metric", "Critical operations","Value", CriticalOps),
      pack("Metric", "High operations",    "Value", HighOps),
      pack("Metric", "Denied operations",  "Value", DeniedOps),
      pack("Metric", "Distinct source IPs","Value", DistinctIPs),
      pack("Metric", "Distinct callers",   "Value", DistinctCallers)
  )
| mv-expand Item = Column1
| project Metric = tostring(Item.Metric), Value = toint(Item.Value)
content.title: At a glance

content.json:
## 1️⃣ Where privileged changes originate — `AzureActivity`

One bubble per source IP. **Size and color = `RiskScore`**, so a single `elevateAccess` from an unfamiliar country outweighs a chatty deployment pipeline. Hover for the tier breakdown (`C/H/M`) and the denied count — a high-denial origin is either a broken pipeline or someone probing what their token can reach.

content.query:
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where CategoryValue == "Administrative"
| where OperationNameValue has_any ("/write", "/delete", "/action")
| where isnotempty(CallerIpAddress)
| extend RiskTier = case(
    // CRITICAL - identity, entitlement, and defense-evasion operations.
    // These grant or hide access; a foreign origin here is an incident.
    OperationNameValue has_any (
        "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/ROLEASSIGNMENTS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/ROLEASSIGNMENTS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/ROLEDEFINITIONS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/ELEVATEACCESS/ACTION",
        "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/POLICYASSIGNMENTS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.SECURITY/PRICINGS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.INSIGHTS/DIAGNOSTICSETTINGS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.OPERATIONALINSIGHTS/WORKSPACES/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.SECURITYINSIGHTS/ALERTRULES/DELETE"
    ), "Critical",
    // HIGH - secret access and network exposure. Key Vault operations and
    // NSG/firewall changes are the classic pre- and post-exploitation steps.
    OperationNameValue has_any (
        "MICROSOFT.KEYVAULT/VAULTS/SECRETS/",
        "MICROSOFT.KEYVAULT/VAULTS/ACCESSPOLICIES/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.KEYVAULT/VAULTS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/NETWORKSECURITYGROUPS/SECURITYRULES/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/NETWORKSECURITYGROUPS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/AZUREFIREWALLS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/PUBLICIPADDRESSES/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.STORAGE/STORAGEACCOUNTS/LISTKEYS/ACTION",
        "MICROSOFT.STORAGE/STORAGEACCOUNTS/REGENERATEKEY/ACTION",
        "MICROSOFT.WEB/SITES/CONFIG/LIST/ACTION",
        "MICROSOFT.MANAGEDIDENTITY/USERASSIGNEDIDENTITIES/WRITE"
    ), "High",
    // MEDIUM - compute/data-plane manipulation and remote execution paths.
    OperationNameValue has_any (
        "MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/RUNCOMMAND/ACTION",
        "MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/EXTENSIONS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.SQL/SERVERS/FIREWALLRULES/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.SQL/SERVERS/DATABASES/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.STORAGE/STORAGEACCOUNTS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.LOGIC/WORKFLOWS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.AUTOMATION/AUTOMATIONACCOUNTS/RUNBOOKS/WRITE"
    ), "Medium",
    "Low")
| where RiskTier != "Low"
| where "{MinRiskTier}" == "Medium"
        or ("{MinRiskTier}" == "High" and RiskTier in ("Critical", "High"))
        or ("{MinRiskTier}" == "Critical" and RiskTier == "Critical")
// Strip any :port suffix on IPv4; leave IPv6 (colon-bearing, no port) intact.
| extend SourceIP = iff(CallerIpAddress matches regex @"^\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+:",
                        tostring(split(CallerIpAddress, ":")[0]),
                        CallerIpAddress)
// Azure-internal callers surface as literal tokens, not addresses.
| where SourceIP !in ("Azure Portal", "Internal", "PolicyEvaluation")
| where SourceIP matches regex @"^\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+$" or SourceIP contains ":"
| extend RiskWeight = case(RiskTier == "Critical", 10, RiskTier == "High", 5, 1)
| extend geo = geo_info_from_ip_address(SourceIP)
| extend Latitude  = toreal(geo.latitude),
         Longitude = toreal(geo.longitude),
         Country   = tostring(geo.country),
         City      = tostring(geo.city)
| where isnotempty(Latitude) and isnotempty(Longitude)
// Aggregate per source IP so the map plots one bubble per origin.
| summarize RiskScore        = sum(RiskWeight),
            Operations       = count(),
            CriticalOps      = countif(RiskTier == "Critical"),
            HighOps          = countif(RiskTier == "High"),
            MediumOps        = countif(RiskTier == "Medium"),
            Succeeded        = countif(ActivityStatusValue == "Success"),
            Denied           = countif(ActivityStatusValue in ("Failure", "Failed")),
            Callers          = dcount(Caller),
            CallerList       = make_set(Caller, 10),
            Subscriptions    = dcount(SubscriptionId),
            RiskyOperations  = make_set_if(OperationNameValue, RiskTier in ("Critical", "High"), 15),
            FirstSeen        = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen         = max(TimeGenerated)
         by SourceIP, Country, City, Latitude, Longitude
| extend HighestTier = case(CriticalOps > 0, "Critical", HighOps > 0, "High", "Medium")
| extend MapLabel = strcat(SourceIP, " (", Country, ") - ", HighestTier,
                           " | ", CriticalOps, "C/", HighOps, "H/", MediumOps, "M",
                           " | ", Denied, " denied")
| project Latitude, Longitude, MapLabel, RiskScore, HighestTier,
          CriticalOps, HighOps, MediumOps, Operations, Succeeded, Denied,
          Callers, Subscriptions, SourceIP, Country, City,
          CallerList, RiskyOperations, FirstSeen, LastSeen
| order by RiskScore desc
content.title: High-risk control-plane origins — bubble size = risk score

content.json:
## 2️⃣ Risk over time

Stacked by tier. A single spike is usually a change window; a sustained Critical band is not.

content.query:
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where CategoryValue == "Administrative"
| where OperationNameValue has_any ("/write", "/delete", "/action")
| where isnotempty(CallerIpAddress)
| extend RiskTier = case(
    // CRITICAL - identity, entitlement, and defense-evasion operations.
    // These grant or hide access; a foreign origin here is an incident.
    OperationNameValue has_any (
        "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/ROLEASSIGNMENTS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/ROLEASSIGNMENTS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/ROLEDEFINITIONS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/ELEVATEACCESS/ACTION",
        "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/POLICYASSIGNMENTS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.SECURITY/PRICINGS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.INSIGHTS/DIAGNOSTICSETTINGS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.OPERATIONALINSIGHTS/WORKSPACES/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.SECURITYINSIGHTS/ALERTRULES/DELETE"
    ), "Critical",
    // HIGH - secret access and network exposure. Key Vault operations and
    // NSG/firewall changes are the classic pre- and post-exploitation steps.
    OperationNameValue has_any (
        "MICROSOFT.KEYVAULT/VAULTS/SECRETS/",
        "MICROSOFT.KEYVAULT/VAULTS/ACCESSPOLICIES/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.KEYVAULT/VAULTS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/NETWORKSECURITYGROUPS/SECURITYRULES/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/NETWORKSECURITYGROUPS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/AZUREFIREWALLS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/PUBLICIPADDRESSES/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.STORAGE/STORAGEACCOUNTS/LISTKEYS/ACTION",
        "MICROSOFT.STORAGE/STORAGEACCOUNTS/REGENERATEKEY/ACTION",
        "MICROSOFT.WEB/SITES/CONFIG/LIST/ACTION",
        "MICROSOFT.MANAGEDIDENTITY/USERASSIGNEDIDENTITIES/WRITE"
    ), "High",
    // MEDIUM - compute/data-plane manipulation and remote execution paths.
    OperationNameValue has_any (
        "MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/RUNCOMMAND/ACTION",
        "MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/EXTENSIONS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.SQL/SERVERS/FIREWALLRULES/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.SQL/SERVERS/DATABASES/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.STORAGE/STORAGEACCOUNTS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.LOGIC/WORKFLOWS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.AUTOMATION/AUTOMATIONACCOUNTS/RUNBOOKS/WRITE"
    ), "Medium",
    "Low")
| where RiskTier != "Low"
| where "{MinRiskTier}" == "Medium"
        or ("{MinRiskTier}" == "High" and RiskTier in ("Critical", "High"))
        or ("{MinRiskTier}" == "Critical" and RiskTier == "Critical")
// Strip any :port suffix on IPv4; leave IPv6 (colon-bearing, no port) intact.
| extend SourceIP = iff(CallerIpAddress matches regex @"^\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+:",
                        tostring(split(CallerIpAddress, ":")[0]),
                        CallerIpAddress)
// Azure-internal callers surface as literal tokens, not addresses.
| where SourceIP !in ("Azure Portal", "Internal", "PolicyEvaluation")
| where SourceIP matches regex @"^\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+$" or SourceIP contains ":"
| extend RiskWeight = case(RiskTier == "Critical", 10, RiskTier == "High", 5, 1)
// Risk over time, split by tier - shows whether an origin is a one-off
// or a sustained campaign. Bin auto-scales with the selected time range.
| summarize RiskScore = sum(RiskWeight) by bin(TimeGenerated, {TimeRange:grain}), RiskTier
| order by TimeGenerated asc
content.title: Risk score over time by tier

content.json:
## 3️⃣ Source IP breakdown

The same data as the map, ranked by risk score. `RiskyOperations` lists the exact Critical/High operations each origin performed — start triage here.

content.query:
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where CategoryValue == "Administrative"
| where OperationNameValue has_any ("/write", "/delete", "/action")
| where isnotempty(CallerIpAddress)
| extend RiskTier = case(
    // CRITICAL - identity, entitlement, and defense-evasion operations.
    // These grant or hide access; a foreign origin here is an incident.
    OperationNameValue has_any (
        "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/ROLEASSIGNMENTS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/ROLEASSIGNMENTS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/ROLEDEFINITIONS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/ELEVATEACCESS/ACTION",
        "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/POLICYASSIGNMENTS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.SECURITY/PRICINGS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.INSIGHTS/DIAGNOSTICSETTINGS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.OPERATIONALINSIGHTS/WORKSPACES/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.SECURITYINSIGHTS/ALERTRULES/DELETE"
    ), "Critical",
    // HIGH - secret access and network exposure. Key Vault operations and
    // NSG/firewall changes are the classic pre- and post-exploitation steps.
    OperationNameValue has_any (
        "MICROSOFT.KEYVAULT/VAULTS/SECRETS/",
        "MICROSOFT.KEYVAULT/VAULTS/ACCESSPOLICIES/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.KEYVAULT/VAULTS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/NETWORKSECURITYGROUPS/SECURITYRULES/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/NETWORKSECURITYGROUPS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/AZUREFIREWALLS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/PUBLICIPADDRESSES/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.STORAGE/STORAGEACCOUNTS/LISTKEYS/ACTION",
        "MICROSOFT.STORAGE/STORAGEACCOUNTS/REGENERATEKEY/ACTION",
        "MICROSOFT.WEB/SITES/CONFIG/LIST/ACTION",
        "MICROSOFT.MANAGEDIDENTITY/USERASSIGNEDIDENTITIES/WRITE"
    ), "High",
    // MEDIUM - compute/data-plane manipulation and remote execution paths.
    OperationNameValue has_any (
        "MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/RUNCOMMAND/ACTION",
        "MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/EXTENSIONS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.SQL/SERVERS/FIREWALLRULES/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.SQL/SERVERS/DATABASES/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.STORAGE/STORAGEACCOUNTS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.LOGIC/WORKFLOWS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.AUTOMATION/AUTOMATIONACCOUNTS/RUNBOOKS/WRITE"
    ), "Medium",
    "Low")
| where RiskTier != "Low"
| where "{MinRiskTier}" == "Medium"
        or ("{MinRiskTier}" == "High" and RiskTier in ("Critical", "High"))
        or ("{MinRiskTier}" == "Critical" and RiskTier == "Critical")
// Strip any :port suffix on IPv4; leave IPv6 (colon-bearing, no port) intact.
| extend SourceIP = iff(CallerIpAddress matches regex @"^\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+:",
                        tostring(split(CallerIpAddress, ":")[0]),
                        CallerIpAddress)
// Azure-internal callers surface as literal tokens, not addresses.
| where SourceIP !in ("Azure Portal", "Internal", "PolicyEvaluation")
| where SourceIP matches regex @"^\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+$" or SourceIP contains ":"
| extend RiskWeight = case(RiskTier == "Critical", 10, RiskTier == "High", 5, 1)
| extend geo = geo_info_from_ip_address(SourceIP)
| extend Latitude  = toreal(geo.latitude),
         Longitude = toreal(geo.longitude),
         Country   = tostring(geo.country),
         City      = tostring(geo.city)
| where isnotempty(Latitude) and isnotempty(Longitude)
// Companion grid: same data as the map, ranked, with callers and the exact
// risky operations each origin performed.
| summarize RiskScore       = sum(RiskWeight),
            Operations      = count(),
            CriticalOps     = countif(RiskTier == "Critical"),
            HighOps         = countif(RiskTier == "High"),
            MediumOps       = countif(RiskTier == "Medium"),
            Succeeded       = countif(ActivityStatusValue == "Success"),
            Denied          = countif(ActivityStatusValue in ("Failure", "Failed")),
            Callers         = dcount(Caller),
            CallerList      = make_set(Caller, 10),
            Subscriptions   = dcount(SubscriptionId),
            ResourceGroups  = dcount(ResourceGroup),
            RiskyOperations = make_set_if(OperationNameValue, RiskTier in ("Critical", "High"), 15),
            FirstSeen       = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen        = max(TimeGenerated)
         by SourceIP, Country, City
| extend HighestTier = case(CriticalOps > 0, "Critical", HighOps > 0, "High", "Medium")
| project SourceIP, Country, City, HighestTier, RiskScore, Operations,
          CriticalOps, HighOps, MediumOps, Succeeded, Denied,
          Callers, CallerList, Subscriptions, ResourceGroups,
          RiskyOperations, FirstSeen, LastSeen
| order by RiskScore desc
content.title: Source IP breakdown (risk, success vs. denied)

content.json:
## 4️⃣ Callers operating from multiple countries

Pivots the same events by identity instead of IP. `MultiCountry = true` inside a short time range is the control-plane equivalent of impossible travel — though a legitimate admin on a VPN will also trip it, so confirm against the IP list before escalating.

content.query:
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where CategoryValue == "Administrative"
| where OperationNameValue has_any ("/write", "/delete", "/action")
| where isnotempty(CallerIpAddress)
| extend RiskTier = case(
    // CRITICAL - identity, entitlement, and defense-evasion operations.
    // These grant or hide access; a foreign origin here is an incident.
    OperationNameValue has_any (
        "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/ROLEASSIGNMENTS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/ROLEASSIGNMENTS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/ROLEDEFINITIONS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/ELEVATEACCESS/ACTION",
        "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/POLICYASSIGNMENTS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.SECURITY/PRICINGS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.INSIGHTS/DIAGNOSTICSETTINGS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.OPERATIONALINSIGHTS/WORKSPACES/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.SECURITYINSIGHTS/ALERTRULES/DELETE"
    ), "Critical",
    // HIGH - secret access and network exposure. Key Vault operations and
    // NSG/firewall changes are the classic pre- and post-exploitation steps.
    OperationNameValue has_any (
        "MICROSOFT.KEYVAULT/VAULTS/SECRETS/",
        "MICROSOFT.KEYVAULT/VAULTS/ACCESSPOLICIES/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.KEYVAULT/VAULTS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/NETWORKSECURITYGROUPS/SECURITYRULES/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/NETWORKSECURITYGROUPS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/AZUREFIREWALLS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/PUBLICIPADDRESSES/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.STORAGE/STORAGEACCOUNTS/LISTKEYS/ACTION",
        "MICROSOFT.STORAGE/STORAGEACCOUNTS/REGENERATEKEY/ACTION",
        "MICROSOFT.WEB/SITES/CONFIG/LIST/ACTION",
        "MICROSOFT.MANAGEDIDENTITY/USERASSIGNEDIDENTITIES/WRITE"
    ), "High",
    // MEDIUM - compute/data-plane manipulation and remote execution paths.
    OperationNameValue has_any (
        "MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/RUNCOMMAND/ACTION",
        "MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/EXTENSIONS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.SQL/SERVERS/FIREWALLRULES/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.SQL/SERVERS/DATABASES/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.STORAGE/STORAGEACCOUNTS/DELETE",
        "MICROSOFT.LOGIC/WORKFLOWS/WRITE",
        "MICROSOFT.AUTOMATION/AUTOMATIONACCOUNTS/RUNBOOKS/WRITE"
    ), "Medium",
    "Low")
| where RiskTier != "Low"
| where "{MinRiskTier}" == "Medium"
        or ("{MinRiskTier}" == "High" and RiskTier in ("Critical", "High"))
        or ("{MinRiskTier}" == "Critical" and RiskTier == "Critical")
// Strip any :port suffix on IPv4; leave IPv6 (colon-bearing, no port) intact.
| extend SourceIP = iff(CallerIpAddress matches regex @"^\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+:",
                        tostring(split(CallerIpAddress, ":")[0]),
                        CallerIpAddress)
// Azure-internal callers surface as literal tokens, not addresses.
| where SourceIP !in ("Azure Portal", "Internal", "PolicyEvaluation")
| where SourceIP matches regex @"^\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+$" or SourceIP contains ":"
| extend RiskWeight = case(RiskTier == "Critical", 10, RiskTier == "High", 5, 1)
| extend geo = geo_info_from_ip_address(SourceIP)
| extend Latitude  = toreal(geo.latitude),
         Longitude = toreal(geo.longitude),
         Country   = tostring(geo.country),
         City      = tostring(geo.city)
| where isnotempty(Latitude) and isnotempty(Longitude)
// Identity view: one row per caller, with every country they operated from.
// Multiple countries for a single identity in a short window is the
// impossible-travel signal on the control plane.
| summarize RiskScore      = sum(RiskWeight),
            Operations     = count(),
            CriticalOps    = countif(RiskTier == "Critical"),
            HighOps        = countif(RiskTier == "High"),
            Denied         = countif(ActivityStatusValue in ("Failure", "Failed")),
            Countries      = dcount(Country),
            CountryList    = make_set(Country, 10),
            SourceIPs      = dcount(SourceIP),
            IPList         = make_set(SourceIP, 10),
            RiskyOperations = make_set_if(OperationNameValue, RiskTier in ("Critical", "High"), 15),
            FirstSeen      = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen       = max(TimeGenerated)
         by Caller
| extend MultiCountry = Countries > 1
| project Caller, RiskScore, Operations, CriticalOps, HighOps, Denied,
          Countries, CountryList, MultiCountry, SourceIPs, IPList,
          RiskyOperations, FirstSeen, LastSeen
| order by MultiCountry desc, RiskScore desc
content.title: Caller identities by risk and geographic spread

content.json:
---

**Tuning notes.** Operations not listed in the `case()` classifier fall into the `Low` tier and are dropped from every widget — periodically review what's landing there so new resource providers don't go unmapped. To let non-KQL analysts adjust the tiers, move the operation lists into a Sentinel watchlist and `lookup` against it instead of maintaining them inline.
